recent_calls.summary: Antonette called to cancel her Monday morning air conditioning appointment and reschedule it for a morning slot on Wednesday, Thursday, or Friday due to her AC producing excess water. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and callback number, and noted that there was no flooding or electrical risk. Antonette declined information about maintenance programs and was asked for feedback before the call ended.

Antonette called to get an update and schedule service for her air conditioner, which is producing more water than normal. The agent confirmed her service details, including address and contact number, and noted her preference for the next available appointment. Antonette declined additional maintenance program information as she is already enrolled and gave positive feedback about the call experience.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to a real agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user, Marquion Williams, called in response to a previous outreach about an in-person interview. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a specific person named Nicole, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called to reach Julie but was informed by the agent Sophie that Julie was unavailable. The user requested to leave a message for Julie before the call ended.
Larry called Budget asking to speak with the finance manager. The agent, Sophie, informed him she could not access billing information but transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user called and requested to speak with a representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent to assist the user further.

The user called to inquire about why their service technician did not submit a report to their warranty company. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent who could better handle the inquiry.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The agent greeted the user and immediately initiated a transfer to a live agent to assist further. The call was successfully transferred without additional conversation.

The call involved Anna from American Home Shield providing dispatch details for an AC service request for Salila Brimley. The agent confirmed the information and arranged for a callback to discuss the April quote. Anna declined additional maintenance program information and gave positive feedback about the AI assistant.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting without any user response or interaction.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called to speak with someone about an inspection. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent and successfully transferred the call. The call ended shortly after the transfer.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent attempted to gather the service address ZIP code but transferred the call before completing the information gathering.

Michelle Hanson called to request a quote and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, handled the call professionally and completed the transfer successfully.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent, Sophie, transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to confirm an appointment scheduled for 10 o'clock and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was completed successfully shortly after the user's inquiry.

Rick, a plumbing inspector, called to reschedule a water heater inspection at 8416 Challenge Court due to no answer at the door. The agent offered available time slots for Monday, July 20th, and Rick confirmed the 10 AM appointment. The agent confirmed the rescheduled inspection and assured follow-up from the team.

The user called to confirm if the part for their repair had been ordered. The agent assured the user that the team would be contacted to confirm the order status and that the user would receive a phone call update. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The agent confirmed Aubrey Yates' appointment scheduled for 12 PM onwards with technician Andrew and explained that the exact arrival time depends on prior appointments. Aubrey was informed about receiving a call and text when the technician is on the way. The call ended with no further questions from Aubrey.

Randy Maison, a plumbing inspector for St. Louis County, reported a code compliance issue regarding a water heater installed in 2022 at 7719 West Bruno Avenue that lacks a proper pan and drain. The agent confirmed the details and informed Randy that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact him soon to address the issue. Randy declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call.

The user, Taylor Higginbotham, called to schedule a routine AC checkup service. The agent confirmed the details, offered available time slots, and successfully booked the appointment for Thursday, July 23rd at 8 AM. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback and expressing thanks.
The user, Luke, called and requested to speak with CG as instructed by Dan. The agent, Sophie, confirmed she was a virtual assistant and then transferred the call to the requested live agent.

The user inquired about pressure checks and refills for mini splits and provided service details including name, address, and contact information. The agent offered available appointment slots, but the user requested cost information and then asked to speak with a live agent, leading to a call transfer. The call ended with the user being transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user called and was immediately transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

Nicole Barnes called to inquire about her appointment window time. The agent was unable to find her appointment in the system and offered to leave a message or transfer her to a live representative. Nicole requested a transfer, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Michael, an escalation specialist from American Home Shield, called to inquire about scheduling a regular service appointment for a non-working oil forced air furnace. The agent noted the request for the earliest available appointment and asked for the dispatch number to proceed with scheduling.

Malia called to set up a work order for AC service related to a home warranty. The agent informed her that the appointment could not be booked directly and that a team member would follow up with her. The call ended after confirming the details and no further notes were added.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another representative.

The call was initiated by the user but ended quickly without the user providing any information or engaging with the agent. The agent requested the user's service address ZIP code to assist further, but the user hung up before responding.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and request for the ZIP code. The user hung up immediately without responding.

Heather called to inquire about the status and scheduling of work on her house, specifically wanting a callback to clarify the time workers will arrive on Monday. The agent documented her concerns and informed her that a representative will call her back on the next business day. Heather expressed some frustration but appreciated the agent's assistance.

The user called requesting customer service and a technician for an air conditioning issue. The agent acknowledged the request but informed the user that it was after regular hours and a live agent could not be connected at the moment. The call ended shortly after without further action.

The user, Kathy Marshall, called to leave a message for Daniel regarding an introduction to CI Web Group and a potential virtual meeting. The agent requested a ZIP code to pass along the message, but informed Kathy that service is not offered in that area. The agent assured that the message would be noted and passed to Daniel.

The user inquired about purchasing spare parts directly from the parts department. The agent explained that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing does not have a retail parts department but can source and install parts through a service call. The agent offered to take the user's details for a follow-up by the team.

The user called Budget's after-hours assistant and initially provided a ZIP code where service is not available. After providing a different ZIP code, the user identified themselves as calling for Shannon Gregory, but the call ended shortly after without further action.

The user called to schedule an appointment for air conditioning service after receiving a text and voicemail. The agent confirmed the service type and asked for a description of the issue. The call ended shortly after without further details.

The user called Budget Air Conditioning after hours and asked to leave a message for Daniel. The agent, Evie, offered to take the message but requested the user's service address ZIP code to proceed. The call ended shortly after without the user providing the ZIP code or leaving a message.

Joseph called to report that his air conditioner is not cooling and needs immediate service. He mentioned this is the third time a technician has visited for the same issue. The agent documented the urgent request and informed Joseph that a technician will follow up on the next business day since it is after hours.

The user, Joseph, called after hours and indicated he was returning a phone call. The agent offered to take a message or assist with questions, but the call ended shortly after without further details or requests from the user.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly after the agent's initial greeting without any further interaction.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
